task_completion.description: Verify that the bcWebCam application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the bcWebCam application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the bottom toolbar with the barcode icon is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom toolbar of the bcWebCam application is visible, showing the barcode icon button located to the left of the gear icon.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Barcode Options dialog has been opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or settings window specifically for 'Barcode Options' is open and active on top of the main bcWebCam application.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Read Linear Barcodes' setting is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Barcode Options dialog, the checkbox explicitly labeled 'Read Linear Barcodes' is checked/ticked.

task_completion.description: Verify that the settings were applied by clicking OK.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshots, the Barcode Options dialog is closed and the main bcWebCam window is shown, indicating the 'OK' button was clicked to apply the changes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to change the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the bcWebCam GUI (opening menus, checking boxes) rather than using terminal commands or scripts to modify configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not attempt to modify the CSV files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editor or spreadsheet application open modifying 'product_barcodes.csv' or 'qr_codes.csv'.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction is with the genuine bcWebCam application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be the actual bcWebCam software, not a mock-up, web browser search, or image editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task description falsely claims that 'product_barcodes.csv' contains Code 128 and Code 39 barcodes; it actually only contains EAN-13 barcodes. The agent does not need to open, read, or modify this CSV file to complete the task. The VLM should solely focus on verifying that the 'Read Linear Barcodes' setting is enabled in the bcWebCam GUI.
